title: ConvertKit vs ActiveCampaign (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare ConvertKit and ActiveCampaign. See pricing, features, and which email marketing tool suits your needs best.
h1: ConvertKit vs ActiveCampaign: Complete Comparison Guide
intro: ConvertKit and ActiveCampaign are both powerful email marketing platforms, but they serve different audiences. ConvertKit is purpose-built for creators and bloggers, while ActiveCampaign caters to businesses requiring advanced automation and CRM capabilities. Understanding their key differences will help you choose the right tool for your specific needs.

comparison_table.tool_a: Visual automations, landing pages, ecommerce, sequences
comparison_table.tool_b: Advanced automation, CRM, lead scoring, SMS, site tracking

choose_a_if: You're a creator, blogger, or course seller looking for an affordable entry point
You need built-in landing pages and ecommerce capabilities for your business
You want to start free with up to 1,000 subscribers before paying
choose_b_if: You need advanced automation workflows and sophisticated lead scoring
A CRM system integrated with your email marketing is essential
You require SMS marketing capabilities alongside email campaigns

faq.a: It depends on your use case. ConvertKit is better for creators and small businesses due to its simplicity and free tier, while ActiveCampaign excels for companies needing enterprise-level automation and CRM integration. ActiveCampaign has higher customer satisfaction ratings (4.1 on Trustpilot vs 3.2), but ConvertKit's creator-focused features make it superior for content creators.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, ConvertKit or ActiveCampaign?
faq.a: ConvertKit offers a free tier for up to 1,000 subscribers, making it free to start. ActiveCampaign has no free tier but starts at $15/month. However, ConvertKit's first paid plan is $29/month, so ActiveCampaign becomes cheaper as you scale both platforms. For budget-conscious startups, ConvertKit's free tier provides the most value initially.

faq.q: Can I switch from ActiveCampaign to ConvertKit?
faq.a: Yes, you can migrate your subscriber lists and email content between platforms. Both tools support standard export formats and integrations that facilitate data transfer. However, complex ActiveCampaign automations may need to be rebuilt in ConvertKit due to different automation architectures, so plan accordingly during the migration process.
